subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Books & Boards
- **1** rulebook
- **1** appendix
- **1** goal board
- **1** bird tray
- **1** scorepad
- **5** player mats

#### Cards
- **170** bird cards
- **26** bonus cards
- **8** goal tiles

#### Tokens & Dice
- **103** food tokens
- **75** egg miniatures
- **40** wooden action cubes
- **5** custom wooden dice

#### Other Components
- **1** birdfeeder dice tower
- **1** first-player token

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Bird deck** — Shuffle all bird cards into one deck.
2. **Bird tray** — Reveal 3 face-up bird cards into the tray.
3. **Token supply** — Place all food and egg tokens in shared supply.
4. **Birdfeeder** — Toss all 5 dice into birdfeeder tower.
5. **Goal board side** — Choose Green side (ranked places) or Blue side (1 point each).
6. **Goal tiles** — Shuffle goal tiles and place 1 random tile on each of 4 goal spaces.
7. **Bonus deck** — Shuffle bonus cards and place deck on table.

#### Goal Board Modes
- **Green side** — Uses 1st/2nd/3rd place competition each round.
- **Blue side** — Awards 1 point per qualifying item each round.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 — Player-Count Reference List (Rulebook Audit)
- **Found variation** — 2-player setup removes 3 tokens of each gem color equivalent in Wingspan terms: remove specific counts of food pools? (No, Wingspan variation is explicit for tokens in Splendor, not Wingspan.)
- **Found variation** — Wingspan excerpt provides explicit setup differences for **2 players** and **3 players** token/noble counts only in Splendor section? (Not applicable.)
- **Found variation (actual Wingspan)** — The provided Wingspan text includes player-count ranges **1–5 players**, but setup package in excerpt does not provide separate hand/resource values by count.
- **Found variation (actual Wingspan)** — Round turns are fixed for all counts (8/7/6/5).

#### All Players
- **Take mat** — 1 player mat each.
- **Take cubes** — 8 action cubes each (same color set).
- **Take bonus cards** — 2 random bonus cards each.
- **Take bird cards** — 5 random bird cards each.
- **Take starting food** — 5 food tokens each: 1 invertebrate, 1 seed, 1 fish, 1 fruit, 1 rodent.

#### Starting Keep/Discard
- **Keep birds** — Keep up to 5 bird cards.
- **Bird cost at setup** — For each kept bird card, discard 1 starting food token.
- **Keep one bonus** — Keep 1 of your 2 bonus cards; discard the other.

#### Hand Privacy
- **Visibility option** — Hand may be private or public by group preference.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Player
- **Random start** — Randomly select first player.
- **Token handoff** — Give first-player token to that player.

#### Game Length
- **Round count** — Game lasts exactly 4 rounds.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise each round.

#### Action Cube Schedule (All Player Counts)
- **Round 1** — 8 turns per player.
- **Round 2** — 7 turns per player.
- **Round 3** — 6 turns per player.
- **Round 4** — 5 turns per player.
- **Reason** — One action cube is used each round to mark end-of-round goal scoring.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose action** — Select 1 of 4 actions: Play Bird, Gain Food, Lay Eggs, Draw Bird Cards.
2. **Resolve chosen action** — Follow its full cost/effect rules.
3. **Activate row powers** — For habitat actions, activate brown powers from right to left.
4. **End turn** — Leave action cube at left side of row (or play-a-bird area), then pass clockwise.

#### Habitat Action 3-Step Pattern
1. **Place cube** — Put action cube on leftmost exposed slot in chosen habitat.
2. **Gain slot benefit** — Resolve printed benefit for that slot.
3. **Run brown powers** — Move cube right-to-left, resolving optional brown powers.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Play a Bird
- **Habitat check** — Bird must be playable in selected habitat row.
- **Egg cost by column** — Column 1 costs 0 eggs; columns 2–3 cost 1 egg; columns 4–5 cost 2 eggs.
- **Food payment** — Pay printed food cost from your personal food supply.
- **Wild icon** — Wild food icon can be paid with any 1 food type.
- **No-food icon** — Crossed-out circle means no food cost.
- **2-for-1 conversion** — While playing a bird only, any 2 food may count as any 1 food.
- **Placement** — Place bird on leftmost exposed slot of legal habitat.
- **Power timing** — Resolve only WHEN PLAYED power (if present).

#### Gain Food (Forest)
- **Take dice** — Remove dice from birdfeeder according to slot value.
- **Gain matching food** — Take food token matching each selected die icon.
- **Split-face die** — Choose one of the two icons, not both.
- **Card-to-food bonus** — If shown, you may discard up to 1 bird card to gain 1 extra food from remaining dice.
- **Power pass** — Activate brown powers in forest row from right to left.

#### Lay Eggs (Grassland)
- **Egg gain** — Lay eggs equal to slot value (plus/minus power effects).
- **Egg limit** — Do not exceed each bird’s printed egg capacity.
- **Color note** — Egg color is cosmetic only.
- **Overflow loss** — Any eggs beyond legal capacity are lost.
- **Power pass** — Activate brown powers in grassland row from right to left.

#### Draw Bird Cards (Wetland)
- **Card gain** — Draw cards per slot value.
- **Source choice** — Draw from deck and/or face-up tray as allowed by action slot.
- **Tray refill** — Refill tray from deck when required.
- **Power pass** — Activate brown powers in wetland row from right to left.

#### Birdfeeder Management
- **Empty feeder** — If tray is empty, reroll all 5 dice.
- **All same face** — If all dice in tray show same face (including single die case) and you are about to gain food, you may reroll all 5 dice first.

#### Token Supply Limits
- **Food limit** — No player cap on food held/cached.
- **Egg limit** — No global egg-supply cap; use substitutes if physically needed.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Habitat Engine Design
- **Forest role** — Primary food engine.
- **Grassland role** — Primary egg engine.
- **Wetland role** — Primary card-flow engine.
- **Compounding pattern** — More birds in a habitat increase action value through brown-power chains.

#### Power Timing System
- **WHEN PLAYED** — Triggers only when the bird is played.
- **WHEN ACTIVATED (brown)** — Triggers during habitat action pass, right-to-left.
- **Optional powers** — Brown powers are optional unless card text says otherwise.

#### Round-End Sequence
1. **Remove cubes** — Clear action cubes from player mats.
2. **Score round goal** — Score current round’s goal on goal board.
3. **Refresh tray** — Discard all face-up tray birds and refill from deck.
4. **Pass first player** — Rotate first-player token clockwise.

#### Goal Board Interaction
- **Green mode** — Direct placement competition (1st/2nd/3rd).
- **Blue mode** — Independent scoring (1 point each qualifying item).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Game end** — End game after Round 4 is complete.

#### Final Score Categories
- **Bird points** — Sum printed points on birds in your tableau.
- **Bonus points** — Add points from your kept bonus card(s).
- **Round goals** — Add points from end-of-round goal scoring.
- **Eggs** — +1 point per egg on your birds.
- **Cached food** — +1 point per food token cached on birds.
- **Tucked cards** — +1 point per card tucked under birds.

#### Winner and Tie-Break
- **Primary winner** — Highest total points wins.
- **Tie-breaker** — If tied, player with most unused food tokens wins.
- **Final tie** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Stabilize one engine first** — Build one reliable row early (usually Forest for food or Wetland for card quality).
- **Prefer cheap utility birds** — Early low-cost birds with brown powers create repeatable tempo.
- **Avoid greedy keeps** — Keeping too many expensive birds in setup can leave you unable to play turns 1–2.
- **Plan egg economy early** — Make sure you can pay egg costs for columns 2–5 before hand clogs.
- **Set round-goal target early** — Decide if this round’s goal is worth contesting before spending key actions.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Action ping-pong** — Rotating all four actions evenly instead of repeating your highest-value row.
- **Egg bottleneck blind spot** — Ignoring egg costs until you can’t play the birds in hand.
- **Over-discarding for food** — Trading too many bird cards for short-term food in Forest.
- **Single-row overbuild** — Overinvesting one habitat while starving food/card support rows.
- **Score-source tunnel vision** — Chasing only bird points and ignoring eggs/cached food/tucked cards/goals.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Engine vs points now** — Decide when to improve a row versus converting actions directly into scoring.
- **Goal race vs tableau quality** — Decide whether to chase Green-board placements or prioritize cleaner private engine growth.
- **Card volume vs playability** — Decide if drawing cards is useful now or if your current food/egg economy cannot convert them before game end.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: provided Wingspan rulebook excerpt. Reformatted for scanability with explicit player-count audit section.
